title: elymus canadensis var. philadelphicus
intro: Elymus canadensis var. philadelphicus, also known as Philadelphia wheat grass or Canada wild rye, is a perennial grass native to eastern North America. The medicinal properties of this plant include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions. The health benefits of Elymus canadensis var. philadelphicus include reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and promoting wound healing. The herbal preparation of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
